title: project-digest: expose --list (already-built function, no CLI flag) and onboard project #2
prompt:
apps/project-digest/lib/config.py already has list_project_ids() (used today only inside an internal error message, config.py:73) but digest.py has no --list flag, so 'what projects can I even ask about' has no answer short of `ls projects/*.json`. Two pieces:

1. Add `python3 digest.py --list` (or `--projects`) that prints the configured project ids (and ideally name + path) as clean text or JSON, wrapping the existing list_project_ids().

2. Onboard a real project #2 per the app's own README 'Adding a project' section — pick a live, git-tracked project from mochaccino/data/constellation.json (David's call which one, default to the next most actively-worked repo if not specified). Follow docs/extension-notes.md's own ranked list of what generalizing needs (marker-vocabulary override, goal-extraction mode, done-convention abstraction) and note honestly which of those three actually needed real code vs worked out of the box.
verify: python3 digest.py --list prints at least 2 project ids; python3 digest.py <project-2-id> --no-write produces a sensible box (or documents exactly what's degraded and why) for the newly onboarded project.
